title: Daemon Operations, Upgrades, Recovery, Cleanup, Orchestration Boundary, and Capstone Defense · Layer and Runtime Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective production Docker operations and release defense; isolate the first causal fault in daemon operations, upgrades, recovery, cleanup, orchestration boundary, and capstone defense and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in production Docker operations and release defense and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback.
Plan Engine, API, containerd, runtime, BuildKit, Buildx, Compose, plugin, storage, and deprecated-feature compatibility across staged upgrade and rollback.
Attribute disk use to images, build cache, writable layers, logs, volumes, and snapshots; preserve owners and backups before scoped cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Daemon Config Live Restore
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug board reviews a digest-pinned multi-service release with an untested rollback, stale backup, daemon upgrade, capacity uncertainty, and unclear ownership. Defend rollout, recovery, compatibility, and residual limits. Competency probe: Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback.

Inspect the Docker Daemon Config Live Restore trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Daemon Config Live Restore.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, dns, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Daemon Config Live Restore, keep Docker Daemon Config Live Restore connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Capstone Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-capstone-production-defense-2o9h0a". Tailor its production-defense.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Capstone Production Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Capstone Production Defense into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Capstone Production Defense, keep Docker Capstone Production Defense connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-capstone-production-defense-2o9h0a
  - competency: docker-capstone-production-defense
    artifact: production-defense.yaml
    decision: capstone production defense
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: restored checksum and application-level consistency query
    case-key: 2o9h0a
    change: |
      release: digest-pinned
      rollout: health-and-observation-gated
      rollback: rehearsed-previous-digest
      backup-restore: timed-and-proven
      upgrade: compatibility-canary
      capacity: measured-limits
      handoff: accessible-runbook
    verify: docker compose config --quiet && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate@sha256:release
    failure: Reject latest-tag rollout, untested rollback, backup without restore, blind daemon upgrade, or a container boundary claimed as orchestrator durability.
    repair: Defend the operating model with immutable identity, canary signals, failure drills, recovery objectives, capacity evidence, ownership, and residual risks.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
